title: OpenRouter API keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: OpenRouter API key detected with sk-or-v1- prefix. OpenRouter API keys provide unified access to multiple LLM providers including OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Meta, and others through a single API gateway. Exposing these keys enables unauthorized API usage across multiple AI services, incurring significant costs, accessing routing configurations, and potentially exfiltrating data through model interactions. Revoke this key immediately from openrouter.ai and use environment variables or secret management systems for secure storage.

OpenRouter API key detected with sk-or- prefix. OpenRouter provides unified LLM routing across multiple AI providers. Exposing these API keys allows unauthorized users to route requests through your account to OpenAI GPT models, Anthropic Claude, Google PaLM, Meta Llama, and other LLM services, incurring substantial costs and potentially accessing your routing preferences and usage data. Revoke immediately and implement secure credential storage.

OpenRouter API key detected in variable assignment. OpenRouter API keys authenticate unified access to multiple LLM providers through a single routing gateway. Exposing these keys in code allows unauthorized users to consume your API quota across multiple AI services, access model routing configurations, and incur substantial costs from GPT-4, Claude, and other premium model usage. Use secure credential management and rotate keys immediately if exposed.

OpenRouter API key detected in Bearer authorization header. Bearer tokens with OpenRouter API keys provide direct access to unified LLM routing services across multiple AI providers. Exposing these keys in authorization headers enables attackers to make unlimited API calls at your expense through OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and other premium LLM providers. Store credentials securely and implement proper access controls.

Generic OpenRouter API key detected in source code. OpenRouter API keys authenticate unified access to multiple LLM providers including OpenAI GPT models, Anthropic Claude, Google PaLM, Meta Llama, and others through a single routing interface. Exposing these credentials enables unauthorized API usage across multiple premium AI services, financial losses from high-cost model consumption, access to routing configurations, and potential data exfiltration through model interactions. Use environment variables or secret managers for secure credential storage and rotate keys immediately.
